requirement.text: System shall allow providers to prescribe medications electronically and select the patient's preferred pharmacy from a nationwide directory.

test_cases.description: Verify provider can electronically prescribe a medication successfully.
test_cases.setup: EHR test environment; test provider logged in; test patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search and select Amoxicillin.
Step: 3. Enter dosage and frequency.
Step: 4. Click 'Sign & Send'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1.
ExpectedResult: 2.
ExpectedResult: 3. Medication order is saved to the patient record.
ExpectedResult: 4. Order status updates to 'Sent' in the clinical workflow.

test_cases.description: Verify system prevents submission when an invalid dose value is entered.
test_cases.setup: EHR test environment; test provider logged in; test patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search and select Amoxicillin.
Step: 3. Enter an invalid dose value (e.g., '-500' or 'abc').
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Medications page within Orders view appears
ExpectedResult: 2. Amoxicillin is searchable and selectable.
ExpectedResult: 3.System displays a validation error message regarding the dose field and the order is not able to be saved or transmitted to the pharmacy.

rationale: The test suite verifies the electronic prescription submission functionality but completely omits testing the selection of a preferred pharmacy from the nationwide directory, leaving a critical functional coverage gap.
description: This requirement may be decomposed into two clear requirement specs: 1) the system shall allow provides to prescribe medications electronically and 2) the system shall allow providers to select the patient's preferred pharmacy from a nationwide pharmacy. The first spec is met via positive and negative testing (TC-HC-001-A, TC-HC-001-B); however, no test cases exist that cover the second spec regarding selecting the preferred pharmacy. The overall disposition is that there exists a significant functional coverage gap.

requirement.text: The EHR shall automatically import heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation (SpO2) from connected continuous vitals monitors every 5 minutes.

test_cases.description: Verify automated vitals polling for heart rate and blood pressure.
test_cases.setup: EHR integration sandbox; continuous vitals monitor simulator connected to patient bed; patient admitted.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Start the vitals simulator broadcasting HR and BP.
Step: 2. Wait 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Refresh the patient's flowsheet in the EHR.
Step: 4. Verify timestamp of the new data entry.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Vitals simulator starts broadcasting HR and BP.
ExpectedResult: 2. Vitals simulator is functionally operable and displays current values.
ExpectedResult: 3. The patient's flowsheet is reloaded containing new values.
ExpectedResult: 4. The new value timestamp corresponds to 5 minutes from last entry.

rationale: The test suite successfully covers heart rate and blood pressure data ingestion at the required interval, but completely misses the functional requirement to import oxygen saturation (SpO2) data.
description: The functional aspects for continuously monitoring heart rate (HR) and blood pressure (BP) are met via TC-HC-003-A and TC-HC-003-B as it is clearly demonstrated each vitals is checked on the requested time interval and successfully refreshed. However, there is no mention of SpO2 monitoring. For this reason, this test suite contains a major functional gap.

requirement.text:
The e-Prescribing module shall require justification from the provider if a dose warning is triggered.

Context: Warnings are triggered if the prescribed dose exceeds FDA recommended maximums. Provided justification shall include medical necessity diagnosis and supporting clinical evidence to request prior authorization.

test_cases.description: Verify the system allows a normal, age-appropriate dosage to be prescribed without warnings.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged into e-Prescribing sandbox; active pediatric patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select Amoxicillin from the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter a standard dosage of 40mg/kg/day for a 5-year-old.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult 1. Amoxicillin is available from the formulary
ExpectedResult 2. Dose is entered.
ExpectedResult 3. Prescription is queued successfully without any warning prompts.

test_cases.description: Verify the system triggers a warning when the maximum dosage is exceeded.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged into e-Prescribing sandbox; active pediatric patient chart open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Select Amoxicillin from the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter a high dosage of 120mg/kg/day for a 5-year-old.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult 1. Amoxicillin is available from the formulary
ExpectedResult 2. Dose is entered.
ExpectedResult: 3. An FDA maximum dosage warning modal is displayed to the provider.

test_cases.description: Verify the system prompts the provider to enter justification if warning is triggered.
test_cases.setup: Provider logged into e-Prescribing sandbox; active pediatric patient chart open; Provider enters a dosage exceeding FDA maximums.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Provider clicks 'Send to Pharmacy'.
Step 2. Enters the justification rationale, attaches medical diagnosis documentation and supporting clinical evidence.
Step 3. Clicks 'Send for authorization'
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. An FDA maximum dosage warning modal is displayed to the provider.
ExpectedResult: 2. The module accepts free text description and uploaded files.
ExpectedResult: 3. Prescription is queued pending insurance authorization for coverage.

rationale: Functional and boundary behaviors (triggering the warning) are tested, but negative coverage is missing for scenarios where the provider attempts to bypass the warning without mandatory justification, or inputs a clearly invalid/alphanumeric dosage.
description: This test suite covers basic functionality of the traced requirement. The requirement may be decomposed into functional elements to 1) confirm invalid doses trigger a warning and 2) require justification in the event of such warning. These functional aspects are covered by TC-HC-012A, TC-HC-012B, and TC-HC-012C; however, there is a negative testing gap. It is not clear whether the module would still queue the high dose prescription for prescription if no attachments were uploaded. For this reason, a negative gap in test suite coverage has been identified.

requirement.text: The pharmacy inventory system alerts staff when the stock of Schedule II controlled substances drops to exactly 10 units or fewer.

description: This test suite covers general functional aspects such as when Schedule II items are above or below the inventory system alert. However, the requirement specifically states that the alert should occur when the inventory drops to 10 units or fewer and this boundary test is not included in the current test suite. For this reason, this test suite has been identified to have a boundary test gap. Furthermore, there is also a negative test gap as all tests are only with Schedule II items. It needs to be confirmed whether a similar test exists with non-Schedule II items to ensure that the alert isnt presented in this scenario if inventory levels are low.

test_cases.description: Verify no alert for high stock levels.
test_cases.setup: Pharmacy inventory module loaded; Schedule II item (Oxycodone) initialized at 20 units.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Dispense 5 units.
Step: 2. Check system alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Remaining stock shows 15 units.
ExpectedResult: 2. No low stock alert is generated.

test_cases.description: Verify alert triggers for very low stock.
test_cases.setup: Pharmacy inventory module loaded; Schedule II item (Oxycodone) initialized at 8 units.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Dispense 3 units.
Step: 2. Check system alerts.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Remaining stock shows 5 units.
ExpectedResult: 2. Low stock alert is actively displayed for the item.

rationale: Missing edge case coverage verifying the exact boundary condition where stock transitions from 11 (no alert expected) to exactly 10 (alert expected).

requirement.text: The EHR system must automatically invalidate the session token and log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity to comply with HIPAA regulations.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: automatic logout after 16 minutes of inactivity.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR; network traffic monitor active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action to register activity.
Step: 2. Leave system completely idle for 16 minutes.
Step: 3. Attempt to navigate to a new patient record.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Session token is invalidated.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is redirected to the login screen.
ExpectedResult: 3. 'Session expired due to inactivity' message is displayed.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: activity at exactly 14 minutes and 59 seconds.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action.
Step: 2. Wait exactly 14 minutes and 59 seconds.
Step: 3. Click a link in the EHR.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Action succeeds.
ExpectedResult: 2. Session timer is reset to 0.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary path: activity at exactly 15 minutes and 1 second.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Perform an action.
Step: 2. Wait exactly 15 minutes and 1 second.
Step: 3. Click a link.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Action is intercepted.
ExpectedResult: 2. User is logged out and redirected to login.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: attempting to reuse the expired token via direct API call.
test_cases.setup: User logged into EHR; API testing tool ready.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Extract active session token.
Step: 2. Wait 16 minutes.
Step: 3. Use API tool to send a GET request for PHI using the extracted token.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized.
ExpectedResult: 2. No PHI is returned, confirming backend token invalidation.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by validating the end-to-end frontend logout, probing the exact temporal boundaries (14m59s vs 15m01s), and securing the negative path by ensuring backend token destruction prevents API bypass, yielding full coverage.
description: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by validating the end-to-end frontend logout, probing the exact temporal boundaries (14m59s vs 15m01s), and securing the negative path by ensuring backend token destruction prevents API bypass, yielding full coverage.

requirement.text: The blood bank inventory system must restrict the allocation of O-negative blood units strictly to patients tagged as 'Emergency Trauma', unless a digital override is executed by a user with the 'Blood Bank Director' role.

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: standard allocation for Emergency Trauma patient.
test_cases.setup: System user logged in; patient tagged as 'Emergency Trauma'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 2 units of O-negative blood for the patient.
Step: 2. Submit allocation request.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System approves allocation.
ExpectedResult: 2. Inventory is decremented by 2 units.

test_cases.description: Verify boundary/negative path: standard allocation attempt for a routine surgery patient.
test_cases.setup: System user logged in (standard nurse role); patient tagged as 'Routine Surgery'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 1 unit of O-negative blood.
Step: 2. Submit allocation request.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System blocks the allocation.
ExpectedResult: 2. Error shown: 'O-negative restricted to Emergency Trauma. Override required.'

test_cases.description: Verify functional path: Blood Bank Director overrides restriction for a routine patient.
test_cases.setup: User with 'Blood Bank Director' role logged in; patient tagged as 'Routine Surgery'.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Request 1 unit of O-negative blood.
Step: 2. System prompts restriction warning.
Step: 3. Click 'Director Override' and submit.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. O-negative unit is requested.
ExpectedResult: 2. Restriction warning presents to user requesting override.
ExpectedResult: 3. Override is accepted - allocation is approved and logged in the audit trail.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: unauthorized user attempts digital override API call.
test_cases.setup: Standard nurse logged in; intercept proxy active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Submit allocation request for routine patient.
Step: 2. Intercept API call and inject {"director_override": true}.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Backend RBAC validation fails.
ExpectedResult: 2. Allocation is denied with HTTP 403 Forbidden.

rationale: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by confirming normal operational logic, testing the boundary of patient tagging restrictions, and ensuring robust negative security testing around Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) overrides, missing no functional dimensions.
description: This test suite fully verifies the requirement by confirming normal operational logic, testing the boundary of patient tagging restrictions, and ensuring robust negative security testing around Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) overrides, missing no functional dimensions.

requirement.text: The telemedicine interface must display a 'Secure Connection' shield icon in the top-left corner of the video window throughout the duration of a live call to confirm active AES-256 encryption.

test_cases.description: Verify indicator presence at session start.
test_cases.setup: Clinician and patient are connected via a new video session.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Access the active video call screen.
Step: 2. Inspect the top-left status bar.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The 'Secure Connection' shield icon is visible.
ExpectedResult: 2. Icon color is green, indicating active encryption.

test_cases.description: Verify indicator persistence during a long-duration call.
test_cases.setup: Video session has been active for 20+ minutes.
test_cases.steps: Step: 1. Monitor the status bar at intervals during the call.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Shield icon remains visible without flickering or disappearing.

test_cases.description: Verify indicator removal upon session termination.
test_cases.setup: Active video session is in progress.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Click 'End Call' to terminate the session.
Step: 2. Observe the transition to the post-call summary screen.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The video interface closes.
ExpectedResult: 2. The encryption indicator is no longer present (session state destroyed).

rationale: This suite achieves 100% coverage because the requirement is a passive UI state triggered solely by the existence of a session. Since there are no numerical variables, input fields, or user-configurable security settings involved, boundary and negative tests do not apply to this specific display logic.
description: This suite achieves 100% coverage because the requirement is a passive UI state triggered solely by the existence of a session. Since there are no numerical variables, input fields, or user-configurable security settings involved, boundary and negative tests do not apply to this specific display logic.

requirement.text: Mental Health session notes must be restricted to the 'Assigned Provider'. All other clinical staff must be blocked from viewing these notes unless they initiate the 'Emergency Break-Glass' protocol, which requires an audit reason.

test_cases.description: Verify positive path: Assigned provider accesses notes directly.
test_cases.setup: User 'Dr. Smith' (Assigned Psychiatrist) is logged in; patient 'John Doe' record is open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Session Notes' section.
Step: 2. Click on the most recent mental health note.
test_cases.expectedResults: ExpectedResult: 1. Note content displays immediately without additional prompts.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: Unauthorized staff access blocked.
test_cases.setup: User 'Nurse Joy' (Not assigned to patient) is logged in; patient 'John Doe' record is open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Session Notes' section.
Step: 2. Attempt to click the mental health note.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Note content is obscured/hidden.
ExpectedResult: 2. System displays 'Access Restricted' message.

test_cases.description: Verify positive path: Emergency access via Break-Glass protocol.
test_cases.setup: User 'Dr. Brown' (ER Doctor, not assigned) is logged in; patient 'John Doe' record is open.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Click 'Emergency Break-Glass' button.
Step: 2. Enter 'Life-threatening emergency' in the audit reason field.
Step: 3. Confirm access.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. Access is granted.
ExpectedResult: 2. System logs the event with the provided reason in the audit trail.

test_cases.description: Verify negative path: Break-Glass attempt without audit reason.
test_cases.setup: User 'Dr. Brown' (ER Doctor) logged in; Break-Glass prompt active.
test_cases.steps:
Step: 1. Leave the 'Audit Reason' field empty.
Step: 2. Click 'Confirm Access'.
test_cases.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. System prevents access.
ExpectedResult: 2. Validation error: 'Reason for emergency access is required' is displayed.

rationale: This suite provides full coverage by testing the authorized access (Positive), the default restriction for unauthorized users (Negative), the override mechanism (Positive), and the enforcement of the audit trail requirement (Negative). Boundary cases do not apply as the access is controlled by discrete user roles and session-assignment flags rather than numerical ranges.
description: This suite provides full coverage by testing the authorized access (Positive), the default restriction for unauthorized users (Negative), the override mechanism (Positive), and the enforcement of the audit trail requirement (Negative). Boundary cases do not apply as the access is controlled by discrete user roles and session-assignment flags rather than numerical ranges.
